response:
<<Word Count Function>>

Section 1  
The function needs to process the input text and the word. It should convert both to lowercase. The function should return the count of the word.  

* * *  

Section 2  
The text may contain punctuation and multiple spaces. The word to search for is a single string. The function should ignore case sensitivity.  

* * *  

Section 3  
The function can use the split method to break the text into words. It should compare each word to the target word. The count should be incremented if they match.  

* * *  

Section 4  
The function should be case insensitive, so both text and word are converted to lowercase. The count is returned as an integer. The function should handle edge cases like punctuation and multiple spaces.  

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections. Mark the beginning of each section with Section X, such as:  
Section 1  
[content of section 1]  
Section 2  
[content of section 2] No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet.  
Section 3  
[content of section 3]  
Section 4  
[content of section 4]
